cards.back: Encryption, firewalls, antivirus software and hierarchical access levels.
A company’s ability to prepare, survive, respond to and recover from a cyber attack.

cards.front: Name three consequences of a cyber attack.
cards.back: Loss of data, corrupted software, websites taken down, loss of reputation/competitive advantage, financial loss.

cards.front: Name three cyber attack prevention controls.
cards.back: Boundary firewall, secure configuration, access control, malware protection, patch management, staff training.

cards.front: Name one cyber attack recovery control.
cards.back: Alternative premises/facilities, what-if scenarios or regular backups.

cards.front: What is a passive digital footprint?
cards.back: Data collected without a user’s knowledge.

cards.front: What is an active digital footprint?
Name three laws in this topic.
cards.back: Data Protection Act 2018 including GDPR, Computer Misuse Act 1990, Investigatory Powers Act 2016.

cards.front: What ethical impacts should learners consider?
cards.back: Impacts on individual privacy and wider society.
